name: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B v1 (Reasoning On)
license: NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.1 Community License Agreement
archDisplay: Dense Transformer, NAS-compressed from Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct (variable FFN + skip-attention + FFN fusion)
note: Flagship open reasoning model derived from Llama-3.1-405B; outperforms DeepSeek-R1 on GPQA/IFEval/LiveCodeBench at under half the size.
tpsNote: Needs ~127GB+ at INT4 — far beyond a single RTX 4090; designed to fit an 8xH100 node.

results.notes: BFCL V2 Live, Reasoning On. Confirmed via raw README.md fetch of the HF model card, which publishes both modes (Off=73.62, On=74.10).

Humanity's Last Exam score from Artificial Analysis's own independent re-evaluation (Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown), read directly off the rendered per-benchmark leaderboard chart on the model's AA page ('Llama Nemotron Ultra' entry = 8%, verified by exact position match against the full 28-model HLE ranking list). AA only publishes a page for the reasoning-enabled variant (the non-reasoning URL 404s), so this applies to the Reasoning On config specifically. Not previously tracked; existing corpus entries for this model only cover MATH500/AIME25/GPQA/IFEval/LiveCodeBench/BFCL from the self-reported HF card.

IFBench (58 new/harder verifiable instruction-following constraints, distinct from IFEval which is already tracked for this model) score from Artificial Analysis's independent Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown, position-verified against the full 23-model IFBench ranking list ('Llama Nemotron Ultra' = 38%, lowest of the 23 shown). Genuinely different benchmark from the corpus's existing 'ifeval' result (88.85/89.45), not a duplicate.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real via HF model card fetch: all 5 self-reported benchmark rows (MATH500 97.00, AIME25 72.50, GPQA 76.01, IFEval 89.45, LiveCodeBench 66.31) match exactly. DeepSeek-R1 GPQA-Diamond comparison figure of 71.5 independently corroborated (though VentureBeat URL itself 403'd on direct fetch — kept as independent row since the number is well corroborated elsewhere). CORRECTED price.provider from 'DeepInfra' to 'NVIDIA API' for the same reason as the Reasoning-Off variant.
